questions.question_text: Which statement about an Alabama qualifying broker is correct?
questions.correct_answer: They supervise all licensees in the company
distractors: They may delegate their supervisory duties to a team leader
They need 12 months of salesperson experience
They are appointed by AREC directly
questions.explanation: The qualifying broker is responsible for supervising ALL licensees in the company and may not delegate that supervisory duty to a team leader. A broker may serve as qualifying broker for more than one company, but only if every company consents in writing, the consent is filed with AREC, and all the companies share the same company address. The 24-of-36-months experience rule is the requirement for obtaining a broker license, not a separate qualifying broker rule.

questions.question_text: At least annually, an Alabama qualifying broker must explain which document to every licensee in the company?
distractors: RECAD Brokerage Services Form
Company trust account statement
Annual license renewal reminder
questions.explanation: The qualifying broker must explain the ADOP to every licensee at least once per year, and each licensee signs an acknowledgment that the broker keeps on file for three years. The ADOP is itself a RECAD mandate, though it is a different document from the RECAD Brokerage Services Form.

questions.question_text: Under what conditions may a broker serve as qualifying broker for more than one Alabama real estate company?
questions.correct_answer: All companies consent in writing, the consent is filed with AREC, and all companies share the same company address
distractors: It is never permitted; a broker may serve only one company at a time
Only if AREC issues a separate qualifying broker license for each company
Only if the companies are located in different counties
questions.explanation: Alabama law allows a broker to serve as qualifying broker for more than one company when all of the companies consent in writing, the consent is filed with AREC, and all of the companies share the same company address. A blanket one-company-only rule is not Alabama law.

questions.question_text: To qualify for a broker license in Alabama, how many months of active salesperson experience are required?
distractors: 12 of the preceding 24 months
36 consecutive months
48 of the preceding 60 months
questions.explanation: An Alabama broker applicant must have held an active salesperson license for at least 24 of the 36 months immediately preceding the broker application. This ensures sufficient real-world experience before taking on supervisory responsibilities.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker must be an authorized signer on which type of account?
distractors: Only the operating account
Only accounts over $10,000
The company's general business account
questions.explanation: The qualifying broker must be an authorized signer on all trust and escrow accounts held by the company. This ensures proper oversight of client funds. The broker is ultimately responsible for trust fund management.

questions.question_text: Which business entity type in Alabama does NOT need a company license to operate a real estate brokerage?
questions.correct_answer: All types require a company license
distractors: Sole proprietorship is exempt
Limited liability companies are exempt
Nonprofit corporations are exempt
questions.explanation: Every real estate company in Alabama — whether a corporation, partnership, LLC, or sole proprietorship — must have a company license issued by AREC. There are no exemptions based on business entity type.

questions.question_text: A brokerage opens a second office in a different city. What does Alabama law require?
distractors: No additional registration is needed
A majority vote of the AREC commissioners
Approval from the local real estate board
questions.explanation: Each branch office requires separate registration with AREC, and each branch must operate under a qualifying broker licensed at that address — no person may serve as qualifying broker at more than one location. As of the 2025 law changes, agents may now use any office of their brokerage to meet clients.

questions.question_text: Under the 2025 Alabama law changes, a salesperson affiliated with a multi-office brokerage can now:
questions.correct_answer: Use any office of the brokerage to meet clients
distractors: Only work from their designated office
Work from any office in the same county
Only meet clients at the main office
questions.explanation: The 2025 license law amendments removed the antiquated prohibition on agents using branch offices other than their assigned one. Agents can now meet clients at any office of their brokerage, improving flexibility.

questions.question_text: Under the 2025 team rules, which statement about real estate teams in Alabama is correct?
questions.correct_answer: No additional team licensing is required
distractors: Teams must obtain a separate team license
Teams must register with AREC annually
Team leaders must hold broker licenses
questions.explanation: The 2025 law established basic team definitions and advertising rules but expressly prohibited requiring additional team licensing. The qualifying broker remains responsible for team supervision and advertising compliance.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker is responsible for which aspect of a real estate team operating under their company?
distractors: Personally closing all team transactions
Setting individual team member commission rates
Recruiting new team members
questions.explanation: The qualifying broker is responsible for supervising team activities and ensuring advertising compliance. The 2025 law clarified these broker responsibilities for teams while establishing basic team definitions. Commission rates are a business decision, not a regulatory requirement.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker passes away unexpectedly. Within how many days must a temporary qualifying broker application be filed?
distractors: 10 days
60 days
90 days
questions.explanation: When a qualifying broker dies or becomes disabled, the application for a temporary qualifying broker must be filed with AREC within 30 days. The temporary QB can operate for up to 6 months, after which a permanent QB must be designated.

questions.question_text: Who can serve as a temporary qualifying broker in Alabama?
questions.correct_answer: A broker or salesperson with 1 year experience
distractors: Only a fully licensed qualifying broker
Any active licensee with no experience needed
Only an associate broker with 2 year tenure
questions.explanation: A temporary qualifying broker can be either a broker or a salesperson with at least 1 year of experience. This provides flexibility when a qualifying broker dies or becomes disabled. The temporary QB can serve for up to 6 months.

questions.question_text: A temporary qualifying broker has been serving for 5 months. What must happen before the 6-month deadline?
questions.correct_answer: A permanent broker must be designated
distractors: The temporary QB becomes permanent automatically
AREC grants an automatic 6-month extension
The company license is simply renewed
questions.explanation: A temporary qualifying broker may serve for no more than 6 months. Before that deadline, a fully licensed permanent qualifying broker must be designated or the company license goes inactive. Extensions are not automatic.

questions.question_text: An associate broker in Alabama holds a broker license but works under a qualifying broker. Their operational role is most similar to:
distractors: An independent broker
A temporary qualifying broker
A branch office manager
questions.explanation: An associate broker holds a broker license but is not the qualifying broker of a company. Operationally, they function like a salesperson — working under a qualifying broker's supervision. They can become a qualifying broker by starting or taking over a company.

questions.question_text: Under Alabama law, which licensee may operate a real estate office out of their home?
questions.correct_answer: A licensed broker in a rural area
distractors: Any licensee whose qualifying broker approves in writing
Any salesperson who registers the home office with AREC
Any licensee living within city limits
questions.explanation: Alabama law permits a home office only for a licensed broker in a rural area, and only if it has a separate entrance and a separate business phone, is not used for living purposes, is used only by the broker, and is identified as a real estate office. Licensees in a city must maintain a separate office within the city limits, though a hardship waiver is possible.
